hints: Bob rerolls when keeping b is worse than a fresh roll. A fresh roll beats Alice w.p. 7/20.
Keep b iff P(A≤b)=b/30 ≥ 7/20 ⇒ b≥11.
solution:
A fresh $d20$ beats Alice (ties to Bob) with probability $\frac1{30}\sum_{a=1}^{20}\frac{21-a}{20}=\frac{210}{600}=\frac{7}{20}$. So Bob keeps a value $b$ iff $P(A\le b)=\frac{b}{30}\ge\frac7{20}$, i.e. $b\ge11$. Then
$$P(\text{Bob wins})=\sum_{b=11}^{20}\tfrac1{20}\cdot\tfrac{b}{30}+\tfrac{10}{20}\cdot\tfrac7{20}=\tfrac{155}{600}+\tfrac{105}{600}=\tfrac{13}{30}.$$
Hence $P(\text{Alice wins})=1-\tfrac{13}{30}=\tfrac{17}{30}.$

hints: By equality of mixed partials, only the count of times each variable is differentiated matters.
Count multisets of size 5 from 10 variables: stars and bars.
solution: Since mixed partials commute, a $5$th-order partial is determined by how many times each of the $10$ variables is differentiated — a multiset of size $5$ from $10$ types. By stars and bars there are $\binom{10+5-1}{5}=\binom{14}{5}=2002.$

hints: Rolls of 1–4 accumulate; the game ends on a 5 (cash sum) or 6 (zero), each equally likely.
Expected accumulated sum E[S]=E[#(1–4 rolls)]·2.5 = 2·2.5 = 5.
Payout = S only if it ends on 5 (prob 1/2): E=5·(1/2)=5/2.
solution: Before stopping, each roll is a $1$–$4$ (continue) with prob $\tfrac46$ or a $5/6$ (stop) with prob $\tfrac26$. The number of accumulating rolls is geometric with mean $\tfrac{2/3}{1/3}=2$, each averaging $2.5$, so $\mathbb{E}[S]=5$. The game ends on a $5$ (cash $S$) vs a $6$ (zero) with equal probability, independent of $S$, so $\mathbb{E}[\text{payout}]=5\cdot\tfrac12=\tfrac52.$

hints: For a doubly stochastic matrix the uniform distribution is stationary (columns sum to 1).
So π_i = 1/100 for all i.
solution: Because every column of $P$ sums to $1$, the uniform vector satisfies $\pi P=\pi$, so the stationary distribution is $\pi_i=\tfrac1{100}$. Then $\|\pi\|=\sqrt{\sum_{i=1}^{100}(\tfrac1{100})^2}=\sqrt{\tfrac{1}{100}}=\tfrac1{10}.$

hints: First reach a head, then wait for the next tail.
E[flips to first H]=2; from a head, E[flips to a T]=2 (each flip ends w.p. ½, else stays in 'have H').
Total = 2+2 = 4.
solution: You need a head followed by a tail. Expected flips to the first head is $2$. Once you have a head, each further flip ends the game if it's a tail (prob $\tfrac12$) and otherwise keeps you in the 'just saw H' state, so the wait for the tail is also $2$. Total $\mathbb{E}=2+2=4.$

hints: Gamma is conjugate to Poisson; posterior is Gamma(a+ΣX, b+n).
Posterior mean = (a+ΣX)/(b+n) = (32+100)/(10+34).
solution: For a Poisson likelihood with a $\mathrm{Gamma}(a,b)$ (shape–rate) prior, the posterior is $\mathrm{Gamma}(a+\sum x_i,\,b+n)$ with mean $\dfrac{a+\sum x_i}{b+n}$. Here $\dfrac{32+100}{10+34}=\dfrac{132}{44}=3.$

hints: Roll until the first value ≥ n, then take it for the remaining actions.
Settled value averages (20+n)/2; expected rolls to reach ≥n is 20/(21−n).
solution: With threshold $n$, you roll until the first value $\ge n$ (expected $\frac{20}{21-n}$ rolls), settling on a value averaging $\frac{20+n}{2}$, then take it for the remaining actions. Expected payout $\approx\frac{20+n}{2}\big(100-\frac{20}{21-n}\big)$. Maximizing over $n$ gives $n=18$: $\frac{38}{2}\big(100-\frac{20}{3}\big)=19\cdot\frac{280}{3}=\frac{5320}{3}.$

hints: Var(e^X)=E[e^{2X}]−(E[e^X])² uses the Gamma MGF M(θ)=(1−θ/3)^{−8}.
Number of digits = ⌊log₁₀(100!)⌋ + 1.
log₁₀(100!)=Σ_{k=1}^{100} log₁₀ k ≈ 157.97 (or use Stirling).
solution: The digit count of $N$ is $\lfloor\log_{10}N\rfloor+1$. Here $\log_{10}(100!)=\sum_{k=1}^{100}\log_{10}k\approx157.97$ (consistent with Stirling), so $100!$ has $\lfloor157.97\rfloor+1=158$ digits.

hints: No rain = neither event; bound P(neither) above.
P(no rain) ≤ min(P(not Sat), P(not Sun)) = min(0.6, 0.3).
solution: No rain means neither day rains, so $P(\text{no rain})\le P(\text{no rain Sunday})=1-0.7=0.3$ (it also can't exceed $1-0.4=0.6$). The tightest upper bound is $\min(0.6,0.3)=0.3$, attained when the dry days are nested.

hints: Beta(1,2): mean 1/3, variance 1/18. Sum 324 of them by CLT.
solution: $\mathrm{Beta}(1,2)$ has mean $\tfrac13$ and variance $\tfrac{1\cdot2}{3^2\cdot4}=\tfrac1{18}$. By the CLT, $S_{324},T_{324}\approx N(108,18)$, so $S-T\approx N(0,36)$ (sd $6$). Then $P(S-T>10)=P\!\big(Z>\tfrac{10}{6}\big)=1-\Phi(\tfrac53)=\Phi\!\big(-\tfrac53\big)$, giving $a=-\tfrac53.$

hints: Compute expected return per dollar staked on each side.
Even: bet 4 to win 6, ½ chance ⇒ return 3 on 4. Odd: bet 6 to win 9, ½ ⇒ 4.5 on 6. Both = 3/4 of stake.
Expected value is 0.75× the stake (a loss), so bet $0.
